DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-28-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. LORNA WALKER, TEL. NO. 1-415-334-1600 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. KRISTI LEMANSKI OF THE PHILLIPS SPALLAS & ANGSTADT LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-278-9400 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT MICHAEL PAGE INTERNATIONAL, INC.'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is overruled. In ruling on a demurrer, courts must take all material facts pleaded as true. (See Sheehan v. San Francisco 49ers, Ltd. (2009) 45 Cal.4th 992, 998.) Here, the Cross-Complaint alleges: (1) Plaintiff and Defendant entered into a contract; (2) Plaintiff breached the contract by failing to perform; and (3) Defendant has been harmed in an amount in excess of $250,000. (See Cross-Complaint 37- 41.) Therefore the first COA has been properly pled and the demurrer is overruled. "There is an implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing in every contract that neither party will do anything which will injure the right of the other to receive the benefits of the agreement." (Comunale v. Traders & General Ins. Co. (1958) 50 Cal.2d 654, 658, internal citation omitted.) Here, Defendant has properly pled that Plaintiff breached the covenant of good faith and fair dealing by interfering with Defendant's right to receive benefits under the agreement. Therefore the demurrer to the second COA is overruled. Finally, Defendant has pled Plaintiff made false representations that Defendant relied upon and as a result Defendant was harmed. Thus, the demurrer to the third COA is overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Suzanne Bolanos; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/sb

MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-28-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. LORNA WALKER, TEL. NO. 1-415-334-1600 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. KRISTI LEMANSKI OF THE PHILLIPS SPALLAS & ANGSTADT LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-278-9400 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANT MICHAEL PAGE INTERNATIONAL, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT is granted in part. Under CCP 436, movant must show the pleading to contain irrelevant, false, or improper matter. As to paragraphs 11, 13-14, 17, and 23 of the Cross-Complaint, the Court finds the Cross-Complaint does not contain irrelevant, false, or improper matter. As to paragraphs 27-36 of the Cross-Complaint, the Court finds the Cross-Complaint does not contain irrelevant, false, or improper matter. Plainitff/Cross-Defendant's motion to strike damages for $250,000 is denied. Plainitff/Cross-Defendant's motion to strike request for attorney's fees is granted with 20 days leave to amend. Order signed in open court. Judge: Suzanne Bolanos; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/sb
